bug_report.summary: Editor viewport/wrapping/scroll metrics differ after pasting identical content (caret position and visible wrapped text change)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application to the main editor window.
Paste the provided multi-block content (CODEBLOCK_A..., 25 filler paragraphs, CODEBLOCK_B... with python code fence) into the new document.
Observe the lower-right editor pane right after the paste completes.
expected_behaviors: After pasting identical content, the editor should land in a consistent caret position and show consistent wrapped lines/viewport for the same window size.
Scrollbar/thumb position and status-bar cursor position should be consistent given identical content and no extra interactions.
actual_behaviors: The lower-right editor pane shows different wrapped text/viewport (e.g., the last visible filler line breaks differently and the caret ends up at a different spot), and the editor’s scrollbar/status indicators differ between before/after captures, despite the same paste input.

bug_report.summary: Ctrl+F opens Find bar but also unexpectedly jumps editor viewport to a different location (CODEBLOCK_B section)
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a long Markdown document with filler paragraphs and two code blocks (as in the test content).
Scroll so a mid-document filler paragraph is visible in the lower-right editor pane.
Press Ctrl+F to open the Find UI.
expected_behaviors: Find UI opens without changing the current scroll position/viewport content (aside from minor vertical shift to accommodate the Find bar).
actual_behaviors: After Ctrl+F, the lower-right editor pane content jumps to a different part of the document (showing CODEBLOCK_B__4A8E11D0 and the python code block), instead of staying at the previously visible filler paragraph area.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: Status bar shows 0 words / 0 characters despite visible content until Ctrl+F is pressed
steps_to_reproduce: Paste a large amount of text (e.g., the provided CODEBLOCK_A + filler paragraphs + CODEBLOCK_B structure).
Observe the lower-right editor pane status bar word/character counts.
expected_behaviors: Word and character counts should reflect the document content immediately after paste and remain consistent when opening Find.
actual_behaviors: Before Ctrl+F, the status bar shows '0 words' and '0 characters' even though multiple paragraphs are visible in the editor. After Ctrl+F, the counts update to non-zero values (e.g., hundreds of words and thousands of characters).

bug_report.summary: Visual artifact/changed rendering on the vertical pane divider/scroll area (small segment differs)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application to the main editor window.
Open the File menu (File → shows dropdown, as in screenshot).
expected_behaviors: The vertical divider/scroll area between panes should render consistently (no extra/missing segment) while menus are open.
actual_behaviors: A small vertical segment on/near the pane divider/scroll area (around x≈895–905, y≈538–584) renders differently after the change (appears as an extra/missing/altered strip compared to before).

bug_report.summary: HTML block comment styling not applied uniformly across all characters (notably around “{}” / “()”), including in Light mode
steps_to_reproduce: Set editor theme mode to Dark.

Enter the block comment:
<!--
Comment line 1: TODO check renderer
Comment line 2: symbols [] {} ()
Comment line 3: 12345
-->
Switch editor theme mode to Light (or Match).
expected_behaviors: The entire comment block (all characters on all lines) uses consistent block-comment styling (monospace/comment color) and a uniform block background across theme switches.
actual_behaviors: Parts of the comment content (especially the “{}” / “()” portion on the symbols line; diff around bbox ~[657,238]-[678,262]) render differently than the surrounding comment text, suggesting the block-comment styling/background is not applied consistently across the full CommentBlock range after the refactor.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering
